text:
Hey, I hear you — some weeks just have it out for you, and there's no use pretending otherwise. When everything seems to go sideways at once, it's exhausting in a way that's hard to explain to people who aren't in it.

But here's what I want you to hold onto: the week is finite. It has an end. Whatever has been piling on you — the frustrations, the disappointments, the moments where you thought "seriously, again?" — none of it is a permanent verdict on your life. It's just a rough patch, and rough patches, by their very nature, do not last.

You've made it through every bad week you've ever had. Every single one. That's actually a remarkable track record when you think about it. Something in you knows how to get to the other side, even when it doesn't feel that way.

And I think the fact that you're still here, still going, still getting up in the morning even when it feels pointless — that takes more strength than people give credit for. Showing up when things are hard is not a small thing.

So take it easy on yourself this week. Let some things be imperfect. Eat something you actually enjoy. Rest without guilt if you can manage it. Be a little kinder to yourself than you think you deserve right now, because I suspect you deserve it more than you know.

Hang in there. The week is almost done, and you're almost through it.
